scenes.title: The Architecture of a Kitsoki Story
scenes.subtitle: Rooms · Phases · Intents · Agent

scenes.body: Free text arrives. A declared, finite alphabet of intents decides what can happen next.
scenes.lede: The LLM only translates — the graph decides.
scenes.narration: Intents decide what happens. The LLM only translates — never acts.

scenes.title: The Architecture at a Glance
scenes.caption: Hosts return typed results via bind: — the machine re-renders the view against the updated World after every bind settles.
scenes.narration: Free text maps to a named intent. Hosts are the only door — results bind back.

scenes.body: The LLM can't invent actions · The machine is pure · The author is in charge
scenes.narration: No invented actions. Pure transitions. YAML is reviewable.

scenes.title: Three State Flavours
scenes.code:
# Atomic — leaf; handles intents directly
kitchen:
  view: "You are in the kitchen."
  on_enter:
    - say: "You smell stew."
  on:
    go_north: { target: hallway }

# Compound — owns children; intents inherit down
ground_floor:
  initial: kitchen
  states:
    kitchen: ...
    hallway: ...
  on:
    quit: { target: menu }   # all children inherit this

# Parallel — regions run concurrently; emit: crosses siblings
world:
  type: parallel
  states:
    lighting: ...   # own intent loop
    calendar: ...   # emit: visible to lighting
scenes.caption: Every room is a state. Not every state is a room — a room is a top-level state the operator can land on.
scenes.narration: Three flavours: atomic leaves, compound groups with inherited intents, and parallel regions that share events across siblings.

scenes.title: One Room's Lifecycle
scenes.caption: No on_exit hook — exit effects belong on the transition that leaves, visible on the outgoing edge.
scenes.narration: on_enter on every arrival. View from world state. Intent routes to a transition. target: picks what comes next.

scenes.title: Six Effect Verbs — The Only Mutators

cards.label: increment
lines: integer delta on a world var

scenes.caption: Effects run in declaration order. Each sees the writes of all prior effects in the chain. The LLM never writes the world.
scenes.narration: Six verbs form the effect alphabet. Set, increment, say, invoke, emit, emit_intent. The LLM never writes the world.

scenes.title: The Turn Loop
scenes.caption: LLM calls and host invocations record their inputs and outputs — every step downstream is replayable.
scenes.narration: Cheap tiers first. LLM only on a miss. machine.Turn is pure and replayable.

scenes.title: The Four-Tier Router

cells: Exact menu string or unique example
Last resort; writes result to cache

scenes.caption: On Oregon Trail, ~3 of 4 turns resolve without an LLM call.
scenes.narration: Deterministic lookup. Synonym bags. Slot-capturing templates. A warm cache. LLM last — writes back to cache.

scenes.title: Agent Verb Surface — host.agent.*

cells: bounded choice → one from a list

scenes.caption: All five route through agent_dispatch.go. extract/decide/ask read-only; task/converse mutate.
scenes.narration: Five verbs ordered by blast radius. Extract and decide are read-only. Task is sandboxed agentic. Converse is persistent.

scenes.title: Meta-Mode — Stepping Off the Graph
scenes.caption: /meta story · /meta kitsoki · bare /meta ask resolves to the story group. /onpath resumes the saved FSM state.
scenes.narration: /meta pauses the FSM and opens a named-agent sidebar. Story and kitsoki groups — edit, ask, and bug verbs. Every app gets them free.

scenes.title: The Gate — Advancing Without Input
scenes.question: What resolves a room's advancing intents?
answer: One advancing intent → auto-advance. The engine fires it; no decider needed.
Multi-way gate → a decider picks: default (deterministic), llm (agent.decide), or human.
emit_intent: dispatches a synthetic intent this same turn — depth-capped at 8.
one-shot mode resolves every gate autonomously; staged mode stops at a multi-way gate for a human.
scenes.caption: The same graph runs fully autonomous, LLM-judged, or human-gated — by flipping execution mode, not rewriting YAML.
scenes.narration: One intent auto-fires. Multi-way gates go to a decider. emit_intent skips the wait.

scenes.title: A Compute Room — No User, No LLM
scenes.code:
build:
  on_enter:
    - invoke: host.run
      with: { cmd: "make build" }
      bind: { build_exit: exit_code }
    - emit_intent: route       # synthetic intent, same turn
  on:
    route:
      - when: "world.build_exit == 0"
        target: deploy
      - default: true
        target: triage
scenes.caption: Guards are pure world.* evaluation via internal/expr. No human, no LLM — same inputs always yield the same branch.
scenes.narration: Bind the exit code, emit route. Guards choose deploy or triage — pure expression.

scenes.title: Phases — Compressing Repeated Rooms
scenes.caption: expandPhases() in internal/app/phases.go materialises the full state graph at load time.
scenes.narration: Declare once. Instantiate per phase. Cycle budgets cap retries. Checkpoint intents let operators jump back.

scenes.title: Story vs Workflow Engine — Rosetta Stone
scenes.caption: A workflow is a story where every gate is single-intent or deterministically guarded — and nothing waits.
scenes.narration: Every workflow concept maps to a story concept. A story adds interruptible gates, typed world, and a decider at every fork.

scenes.title: Story vs Lead Agent + Subagents

left.label: Lead Agent
lines: Plan lives in the model's head

scenes.caption: agent.task ≈ a subagent call. agent.decide ≈ the lead picks which subagent. The graph holds the orchestration.
scenes.narration: The agent decides at runtime. The story decides at author-time. The model is called at declared points.

scenes.tagline: Deterministic graphs. Declared authority. Every decision recorded.
scenes.narration: Deterministic graphs. Declared authority. Every decision recorded.
